title: scan_block snapshot+restore on save failure: correct crash-safety design
description: scanner.rs scan_block takes a snapshot of tree and witnesses before processing each block and restores them if save_block_state fails. This ensures the next scan_to_tip call re-processes the same block against a consistent in-memory tree. A contributor might remove the snapshot to save memory, not realizing it would cause permanent tree divergence after any transient DB error. The snapshot-restore is a correctness invariant, not a performance nicety.
